context: Tests whether the agent produces a 4-tier pattern strategy from the speaker's pattern profile, with antipatterns tagged as [RECURRING] or [CONTEXTUAL] based on the speaker's history.

checklist.name: 4-tier structure present
checklist.description: The report organizes pattern recommendations into exactly four labeled tiers: Signature (or YOUR TOOLKIT), Contextual (or WORTH CONSIDERING), New to You (or NEW TO YOU), and Shake It Up (or WILD CARD) — four distinct sections, not three or five

checklist.name: Signature tier from profile
checklist.description: The Signature tier contains patterns from the speaker's signature_patterns (narrative-arc, foreshadowing, brain-breaks, bookends) — not invented patterns

checklist.name: Contextual tier from profile
checklist.description: The Contextual tier contains at least one pattern from the speaker's contextual_patterns (expansion-joints, a-la-carte-content, talklet)

checklist.name: Shake It Up has 1-2 items
checklist.description: The Shake It Up / Wild Card tier contains exactly 1 or 2 pattern suggestions — not 0 and not 3 or more

checklist.name: [RECURRING] tag used
checklist.description: At least one antipattern detection is labeled [RECURRING] (matching shortchanged or bullet-riddled-corpse from the speaker's antipattern_frequency history)

checklist.name: [CONTEXTUAL] tag used
checklist.description: At least one antipattern detection is labeled [CONTEXTUAL] — a risk detected in the current outline that is NOT in the speaker's historical antipattern_frequency (e.g., meme accretion in the opening sequence)

checklist.name: New to You from never_used
checklist.description: The New to You tier suggests at least one pattern from the speaker's never_used_patterns list — not patterns already in signature or contextual tiers

checklist.name: Actionable recommendations
checklist.description: At least 2 pattern recommendations include specific advice for how to apply them to THIS talk — not generic definitions of what the pattern is
